blockingIssues.description: Line 107: ExtractionConfig defaults store_path to `.skills` but AGENTS.md specifies user-extensible skills should be in `.swell/skills/`. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).
Line 139: TrajectoryAnalyzer has #[allow(dead_code)] attribute and its store field is never used for database lookups. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).
Line 955: Confidence calculation uses sum of all pattern frequencies (frequency_map.values().sum()) as denominator instead of per-pattern frequency. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).

Line 1145: Convention parsing uses wrong string slice index [3..] instead of [4..]. For input '- **name**: description', position 0='-', 1=' ', 2='*', 3='*', 4='n'. Name starts at index 4. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).
Line 1173: get_anti_patterns_for_error is an unimplemented stub returning empty vec with comment 'For MVP, return empty'. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).
blockingIssues.evidence: crates/swell-memory/src/pattern_learning.rs lines 1173-1176: Ok(Vec::new()) with 'For MVP, return empty' comment

blockingIssues.description: BGE cross-encoder reranking NOT implemented. The feature description states 'BGE-reranker for cross-encoder reranking' but no ONNX model loading or BGE inference is present. RerankerModelType::Bge variant exists but falls through to SimpleReranker. No actual BGE implementation exists - Bge type is dead code. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).

MemoryEntry has no confidence field. The feature requires checking confidence < 0.3 to mark as deprecated, but MemoryEntry (swell-core/src/traits.rs:196) has no confidence field. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).
blockingIssues.evidence: swell-core/src/traits.rs MemoryEntry struct definition lacks confidence field

blockingIssues.description: SqliteMemoryStore has no deprecation columns. The CREATE TABLE memory_entries statement does not include: is_deprecated, superseded_by, deprecation_reason, deprecated_at. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).
blockingIssues.evidence: crates/swell-memory/src/lib.rs line 182: init_schema() CREATE TABLE has no deprecation columns

blockingIssues.description: Deprecatable trait is defined but never implemented for any type. The trait requires get_confidence() but MemoryEntry has no confidence field. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).
blockingIssues.evidence: crates/swell-memory/src/deprecation.rs line 130: pub trait Deprecatable with no implementations

blockingIssues.description: check_deprecation() and apply_confidence_deprecation() are never called from anywhere in the codebase outside their own tests. Dead code. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).
blockingIssues.evidence: grep -r 'check_deprecation|apply_confidence_deprecation' shows no calls outside test modules

blockingIssues.description: log_observation() method is missing. EventType::Observation and ObservationPayload are defined but no convenience method exists to log observations, contradicting expectedBehavior 'Stores observations and outcomes immutably'. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).
blockingIssues.evidence: crates/swell-memory/src/event_log.rs: EventLog impl block has no log_observation() method

blockingIssues.description: unwrap_or_default() silently swallows JSON serialization errors in all five convenience logging methods (log_tool_invocation, log_decision, log_error, log_outcome, log_state_transition). An empty {} would be logged instead of the actual event, corrupting the audit trail. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).

get_by_type(), get_by_label(), get_by_provenance() do NOT filter by repository - return ALL memories across ALL repositories. This violates 'Repository scope required for all memory operations' and 'Cross-repo memories isolated by default'. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).

MemoryQuery.search() does NOT filter by source_episode_id despite the field being added to MemoryQuery. The expectedBehavior 'Query memories by provenance' is not fulfilled. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).
blockingIssues.evidence: crates/swell-memory/src/lib.rs line 732: search SQL query never filters on source_episode_id

blockingIssues.description: store() method does NOT persist provenance fields (source_episode_id, evidence, provenance_context). The INSERT statement is missing these columns. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).
blockingIssues.evidence: crates/swell-memory/src/lib.rs line 587: INSERT statement missing source_episode_id, evidence, provenance_context

blockingIssues.description: BM25 search stream generates random UUIDs (Uuid::new_v4()) instead of using actual RecallResult entry_id field. This breaks RRF fusion since IDs don't match actual memory entries. STILL NOT FIXED since round 1 (now round 11).
blockingIssues.evidence: crates/swell-memory/src/triple_stream.rs line 620: Uuid::new_v4() instead of RecallResult.log.id

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: CRITICAL: 17 blocking issues have persisted for 11 rounds without resolution. The orchestrator should create HIGH-PRIORITY dedicated fix features for each blocking issue that get auto-assigned. These are well-identified, localized bugs that should be fixed by targeted features, not expect implementation workers to self-correct. The current approach of re-running scrutiny on the same features without focused fixes has failed for 11 consecutive rounds.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All 17 issues marked 'STILL NOT FIXED since round 1' with file:line evidence. No code changes made to address these issues across 11 rounds.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on error handling for audit-critical paths: unwrap_or_default() is inappropriate for audit trails where data loss is unacceptable. Workers should use ? propagation or handle errors explicitly rather than silently discarding them. This anti-pattern appears in mem-event-log-jsonl across 5 locations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mem-event-log-jsonl review identified 5 unwrap_or_default() calls that silently lose audit data on serialization failure

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on repository-scoped memory operations: all MemoryStore trait methods that return multiple entries (search, get_by_type, get_by_label, get_by_provenance) should accept repository parameter to enforce cross-repo isolation. The trait should be updated to include repository scope.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mem-scoping review identified that get_by_type/get_by_label/get_by_provenance don't filter by repository despite search() doing so

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on trait completeness: features that add methods to existing traits should verify the trait is implemented and integrated. The Deprecatable trait is defined but has no implementations - either implement it or remove the dead code.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mem-deprecation review identified Deprecatable trait with no implementations and MemoryEntry lacking confidence field

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the skill store path convention: skill extraction outputs to `.skills/` but AGENTS.md documents `.swell/skills/` as the user-extensible skill directory. Either (a) skill_extraction.rs should use `.swell/skills/` as the default store path, or (b) AGENTS.md should document both locations with different purposes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: memory-skill-extraction stores to `.skills/` but AGENTS.md line ~210 states 'Users can add new skills to .swell/skills/'

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Standardize error types: event_log.rs uses io::Error directly instead of SwellError or thiserror enum. Architecture.md states 'Error handling via thiserror enums' and AGENTS.md says 'All fallible operations return Result<T, SwellError>'. Workers should follow this convention.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mem-event-log-jsonl review noted io::Error usage vs project convention

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on verifying partial implementations: Workers should not assume a feature is 'already implemented' based on type definitions alone. The expectedBehavior requires actual methods that fulfill those behaviors - if log_observation() is specified but missing, that's a blocking gap regardless of type definitions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mem-episodic-memory worker incorrectly claimed feature was 'already implemented' when only types were defined but methods were missing

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on implementing feature complete: Workers implementing features should verify ALL expectedBehavior items are actually implemented. The mem-deprecation worker only added the module but did not integrate it with SqliteMemoryStore or add confidence field to MemoryEntry - the feature is incomplete but tests pass.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mem-deprecation worker added deprecation.rs module but did not update MemoryEntry or SqliteMemoryStore to support deprecation

rejectedObservations.reason: This is a fact about missing infrastructure, not guidance to apply. The orchestrator owns .factory/ directory.

rejectedObservations.observation: Feature skillName 'rust-worker' vs available 'rust' skill mismatch
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a feature configuration issue, not a guidance issue. The orchestrator manages feature definitions.
